questions.question: The standard meridian of India is:
questions.explanation: The standard meridian of India is 82°30'E (passing through Mirzapur, UP), which is used to determine Indian Standard Time (IST = UTC+5:30).

questions.question: India has the world's largest reserves of:
options: Thorium (in monazite sand, coastal Kerala and Tamil Nadu)
questions.explanation: India has the world's largest known reserves of thorium — found in monazite sands on the Kerala and Tamil Nadu coasts (Chavara, Kerala; Manavalakurichi, Tamil Nadu). India also has 4th largest coal reserves globally.

questions.question: The 'Oil and Natural Gas Corporation' (ONGC) produces most of India's petroleum from:
options: Mumbai High (offshore, Arabian Sea) — about 60% of production
questions.explanation: Mumbai High (Bombay High) offshore oilfields in the Arabian Sea produce about 60% of India's total crude oil. Other producing areas: Gujarat (Ankleshwar, Kalol), Assam (Digboi — oldest oilfield in Asia, 1889), Rajasthan (Barmer/Mangala).

questions.question: The Silent Valley National Park (Kerala) is famous for:
options: Last undisturbed tropical evergreen forest and lion-tailed macaques
questions.explanation: Silent Valley National Park in Palakkad district, Kerala, in the Nilgiri Hills is famous for being the last undisturbed tropical moist evergreen forest in South India, home to the endangered lion-tailed macaque.

questions.question: Gir National Park (Gujarat) is the only habitat in the world for:
questions.explanation: Gir Forest National Park and Wildlife Sanctuary in Gujarat is the world's only natural habitat of the Asiatic Lion. The lion population has grown from ~200 in the 1970s to over 670 now.

questions.question: The Kolar Gold Fields (KGF) are in:
questions.explanation: Kolar Gold Fields (KGF) are in Kolar district, Karnataka. Once the world's deepest gold mines, they produced gold for over 120 years before being closed in 2001 by the government due to non-viability.

questions.question: Which state has the largest reserves of iron ore in India?
questions.explanation: Odisha has the largest reserves of iron ore in India (about 33% of total). Major mines: Barbil-Sundergarh region. Karnataka (Bellary-Hospet) and Chhattisgarh-Jharkhand are also major producers.

questions.question: Singareni Coalfields are in:
questions.explanation: Singareni Collieries Company Limited (SCCL) operates coal mines in Telangana (formerly Andhra Pradesh). Singareni is the only government-owned coal company owned jointly by the State (51%) and Centre (49%).

questions.question: Which state is the leading producer of petroleum (crude oil) in India?
questions.explanation: Gujarat is the leading producer of crude oil in India, with major offshore fields in the Arabian Sea (Bombay High, operated by ONGC) and onshore fields in Ankleshwar. Rajasthan (Barmer) has emerged as a major onshore producer.

questions.question: The Montreal Protocol (1987) aimed to phase out:
options: Chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs) that deplete the ozone layer
questions.explanation: The Montreal Protocol on Substances that Deplete the Ozone Layer (1987) is a landmark treaty phasing out CFCs (chlorofluorocarbons) and other ozone-depleting substances. It is considered the most successful environmental treaty.

questions.question: According to the 2011 census, India's population was approximately:
questions.explanation: India's population was 1.21 billion (121 crore) as per the 2011 census. India is projected to overtake China as the world's most populous country — which happened around 2023.

questions.question: India's Silicon Valley is:
questions.explanation: Bengaluru (Bangalore) is called India's Silicon Valley due to concentration of IT companies — Infosys, Wipro, HAL, ISRO are headquartered here. It is also called the 'Pub Capital', 'Garden City', 'Startup Capital of India'.

questions.question: Evergreen Revolution aims at:
options: Sustainable increase in agricultural productivity without environmental damage
questions.explanation: Evergreen Revolution (concept by M.S. Swaminathan) aims at sustainable increase in agricultural productivity without ecological harm — combining the productivity of the Green Revolution with environmental sustainability.

questions.question: OPEC (Organization of Petroleum Exporting Countries) headquarters is in:
questions.explanation: OPEC is headquartered in Vienna, Austria. Founded in Baghdad in 1960, it currently has 13 member countries. Saudi Arabia is the de facto leader. OPEC controls about 40% of world oil production.

questions.question: The G20 is a group of:
questions.explanation: G20 consists of 19 major economies (Argentina, Australia, Brazil, Canada, China, France, Germany, India, Indonesia, Italy, Japan, South Korea, Mexico, Russia, Saudi Arabia, South Africa, Turkey, UK, USA) plus the European Union.

questions.question: The 'continental shelf' is:
options: The submerged extension of a continent under the shallow sea (up to 200 m depth)
A mountain range under the sea
questions.explanation: The continental shelf is the gently sloping submerged extension of a continent from the shoreline to a depth of about 200 m. Beyond it is the steeper continental slope, then the ocean floor/abyssal plain.

questions.question: The Strait of Malacca connects:
options: Indian Ocean and South China Sea (between Malaysia and Indonesia)
Bay of Bengal and South China Sea
questions.explanation: The Strait of Malacca (between Malaysia/Singapore and Sumatra/Indonesia) is one of the world's most important shipping lanes, connecting the Indian Ocean with the South China Sea and Pacific Ocean.

questions.question: The 'Chipko Movement' was related to:
options: Forest conservation (hugging trees to prevent felling) in Uttarakhand
questions.explanation: The Chipko Movement (1973) in Uttarakhand was a nonviolent protest where villagers (led by Sundarlal Bahuguna and Chandi Prasad Bhatt) hugged trees to prevent logging. It influenced India's forest policy.

questions.question: The Indian Meteorological Department (IMD) is headquartered in:
questions.explanation: IMD (India Meteorological Department) is headquartered in Pune, Maharashtra (Shivajinagar). It was established in 1875 and is the principal government agency for weather forecasting and seismology.

questions.question: The Konkan Railway runs along:
options: Maharashtra, Goa, and Karnataka coasts (Mumbai to Mangalore)
East Coast from Chennai to Odisha
questions.explanation: Konkan Railway (760 km) was built 1990-1998 along the western coast through Maharashtra, Goa, and Karnataka, connecting Roha (near Mumbai) to Mangalore. It passes through 2,000+ bridges and 91 tunnels.

questions.question: The capital of Brazil is:
questions.explanation: Brasília is the capital of Brazil — a planned capital built in just 41 months and inaugurated in 1960. Before Brasília, Rio de Janeiro was the capital. São Paulo is Brazil's largest city and economic center.

questions.question: The capital of Japan is:
questions.explanation: Tokyo is the capital and largest city of Japan (population ~14 million city, ~38 million metro area — world's largest metropolitan area). Before 1868, Kyoto was Japan's capital.

questions.question: Which is the only state in India with two capitals (summer and winter)?
options: Himachal Pradesh (Shimla in summer, Dharamsala or Shimla all year; J&K — Srinagar/Jammu)
questions.explanation: J&K had two capitals — Srinagar (summer) and Jammu (winter) — before becoming a UT. After J&K reorganization (2019), both Srinagar and Jammu serve as capitals of the J&K UT. Himachal Pradesh's capital is only Shimla.

questions.question: Chandigarh is the capital of:
options: Punjab and Haryana both (and a Union Territory itself)
questions.explanation: Chandigarh is a Union Territory that serves as the shared capital of both Punjab and Haryana. Designed by Le Corbusier, it was established after Partition (Lahore went to Pakistan) as Punjab's new capital.

questions.question: What is 'soil erosion' primarily caused by?
options: Deforestation, overgrazing, and poor farming practices — along with wind and water
questions.explanation: Soil erosion is the removal of topsoil by wind, water, or human activities. Major causes: deforestation (tree roots hold soil), overgrazing, poor agricultural practices (no crop rotation, monoculture), and construction.

questions.question: 'Jhum cultivation' (shifting cultivation) is mainly practiced in:
options: Northeast India (Assam, Meghalaya, Nagaland, Mizoram, Manipur, Arunachal Pradesh)
questions.explanation: Jhum (shifting/slash-and-burn) cultivation is practiced in Northeast India — also called 'Bewar/Dahia/Penda' in central India. Forest is cleared, cultivated for 2-3 years, then abandoned to let it regenerate.

questions.question: The 'Dokar La' Pass and 'Jelep La' Pass are on the border of:
questions.explanation: Jelep La Pass (4,270 m) is on the Sikkim-China (Tibet) border and was a major trade route before 1962. Nathu La (4,310 m) is the currently open border crossing between Sikkim and Tibet.

questions.question: India's total forest cover is approximately (as per FSI 2021):
options: About 24% of India's total geographical area
questions.explanation: India's forest and tree cover is about 24.62% of total geographical area as per Forest Survey of India (FSI) State of Forest Report 2021. The National Forest Policy (1988) mandates 33% forest cover — this target has not been achieved.

questions.question: The 'Dongria Kondh' tribal community opposes mining in:
options: Niyamgiri Hills, Odisha (Vedanta/Sterlite bauxite mining case)
questions.explanation: Dongria Kondh tribe of Niyamgiri Hills (Odisha) successfully opposed Vedanta's bauxite mining project through a historic gram sabha vote in 2013, citing the hills as sacred. Supreme Court upheld their right under Forest Rights Act.

questions.question: India's exclusive economic zone (EEZ) extends up to how many nautical miles from its coast?
questions.explanation: India's EEZ extends 200 nautical miles from the coast. India has sovereign rights over the resources (fishing, minerals, oil) in this zone.

questions.question: OPEC (Organisation of Petroleum Exporting Countries) was founded in:
questions.explanation: OPEC was founded in 1960 in Baghdad by Iran, Iraq, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela. Today it has 13 members. OPEC controls ~40% of world oil production. Its secretariat is in Vienna, Austria.

questions.question: The Amazon rainforest is primarily in:
questions.explanation: The Amazon rainforest (world's largest tropical rainforest, ~5.5 million sq km) is primarily in Brazil (60%), plus Peru, Colombia, Venezuela, Ecuador, Bolivia, and other South American countries.

questions.question: India's longest national highway is:
questions.explanation: NH-44 (3,745 km) from Srinagar to Kanyakumari via Delhi-Agra-Nagpur-Bangalore-Chennai is India's longest national highway, running the full north-south length of India.

questions.question: The deepest port in India is:
questions.explanation: Visakhapatnam Port (Vizag) is India's deepest natural port. It has a natural draft of about 17.5 m and is a major port on the east coast. It handles iron ore, coal, and petroleum products.

questions.question: The 'Terai' zone in northern India is:
options: A marshy, forested, low-land zone at the base of the Himalayas (south of Bhabar)
questions.explanation: Terai (from Sanskrit 'low land') is a belt of marshy, forested, low-lying land in northern India and Nepal, south of the Bhabar zone and Shivalik Hills. Underground streams resurface here, creating swampy conditions.

questions.question: The Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant (Tamil Nadu) was built with assistance from:
questions.explanation: Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant in Tirunelveli district, Tamil Nadu, was built with Russia's technical and financial assistance. It has two VVER-1000 reactors, each of 1,000 MW. More units are under construction.

questions.question: What is the primary factor influencing the formation of monsoon patterns in the Indian subcontinent?
questions.explanation: Pressure differences between land and sea drive monsoon patterns. Low pressure over land and high pressure over the ocean create a pressure gradient.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is known for flowing through the Grand Canyon?
questions.explanation: The Colorado River flows through the Grand Canyon. It is a major river in the southwestern United States.

questions.question: What is the term for the process by which water moves through a plant, from the roots to the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the process of water movement through plants. It occurs mainly through stomata on leaves.

questions.question: Which mountain range runs along the border between France and Spain?
questions.explanation: The Pyrenees mountain range forms the border between France and Spain. It stretches for about 435 km.

questions.question: What is the largest desert in the world, covering over 9 million square kilometers?
questions.explanation: The Antarctic Desert is the largest desert, covering the entire continent of Antarctica. It is also the coldest desert.

questions.question: Which strait separates the continents of Asia and Africa?
questions.explanation: The Bab-el-Mandeb strait connects the Red Sea to the Gulf of Aden, separating Asia from Africa.

questions.question: What is the process by which the Earth's plates move and change, resulting in the creation of mountains and volcanoes?
questions.explanation: Plate tectonics is the process of plate movement. It shapes the Earth's surface, creating mountains and volcanoes.

questions.question: Which river is the longest in South America, flowing through Brazil, Peru, and Colombia?
questions.explanation: The Amazon River is the longest in South America, stretching over 6,400 km. It flows through several countries.

questions.question: What is the term for a body of water that is partially enclosed by land, with a restricted connection to the open ocean?
questions.explanation: A sea is a body of water partially enclosed by land. It has a restricted connection to the open ocean.

questions.question: Which climate pattern is characterized by high temperatures and high levels of precipitation throughout the year?
questions.explanation: Tropical climates are hot and humid, with high temperatures and precipitation year-round. They are found near the equator.

questions.question: What is the primary cause of the formation of the Indian monsoon?
questions.explanation: Temperature difference between land and sea is the primary cause of the Indian monsoon.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers flows through the Rift Valley?
questions.explanation: The Nile river flows through the Rift Valley.

questions.question: Which of the following straits separates the continents of Asia and Africa?
questions.explanation: The Bab-el-Mandeb strait separates Asia and Africa.

questions.question: Which river is the longest in South America, stretching over 6,800 kilometers?
questions.explanation: The Amazon river is the longest in South America.

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of people from rural areas to cities, resulting in urbanization?
questions.explanation: Migration is the movement of people from rural areas to cities.

questions.question: Which of the following climate types is characterized by high temperatures and high levels of rainfall throughout the year?
questions.explanation: Tropical climate is characterized by high temperatures and rainfall.

questions.question: Which mountain range acts as a water divide between the Ganges and Indus river systems?
questions.explanation: The Himalayan range separates these river systems.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers flows through the Grand Canyon?
questions.explanation: The Colorado River flows through the Grand Canyon.

questions.question: What is the largest living structure on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Great Barrier Reef is the largest living structure.

questions.question: What is the process by which the Earth's plates move and change, resulting in the creation of mountain ranges?
questions.explanation: Plate tectonics is the process of Earth's plate movement.

questions.question: Which type of rock is formed from the cooling and solidification of magma?
questions.explanation: Igneous rock is formed from cooled magma.

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of water from the ground to the atmosphere through plants?
questions.explanation: Evapotranspiration is the movement of water through plants to the atmosphere.

questions.question: Which of the following is a consequence of deforestation?
questions.explanation: Soil erosion is a consequence of deforestation.

questions.question: What is the primary cause of the formation of the Himalayan mountain range?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas were formed due to the collision of the Indian and Eurasian tectonic plates, a process known as Plate Tectonics.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is known for its unique phenomenon of reversing its flow direction during the monsoon season?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra river reverses its flow direction during the monsoon season due to the heavy rainfall.

questions.question: What is the term used to describe the process by which the Earth's surface is worn away by natural forces such as wind and water?
questions.explanation: Denudation refers to the process of wearing away the Earth's surface.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest hot desert in the world, covering much of North Africa?
questions.explanation: The Sahara is the largest hot desert in the world.

questions.question: Which of the following types of climate is characterized by high temperatures and high levels of precipitation throughout the year?
questions.explanation: Tropical climate is characterized by high temperatures and high precipitation.

questions.question: What is the term used to describe the movement of water from the ground to the atmosphere through plants?
questions.explanation: Transpiration refers to the movement of water from the ground to the atmosphere through plants.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain ranges runs along the border between France and Spain?
questions.explanation: The Pyrenees mountain range runs along the border between France and Spain.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest island in the Mediterranean Sea?
questions.explanation: Sicily is the largest island in the Mediterranean Sea.

questions.question: What is the primary cause of the southwest monsoon in India?
questions.explanation: The primary cause of the southwest monsoon in India is the pressure gradient between the Indian Ocean and the Indian subcontinent.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is not a tributary of the Ganges river?
questions.explanation: Mahanadi river is not a tributary of the Ganges river, it flows into the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Which mountain range runs along the border between India and Pakistan?
questions.explanation: The Karakoram mountain range runs along the border between India and Pakistan.

questions.question: What is the name of the strait that separates the islands of Java and Sumatra?
questions.explanation: The Sunda Strait separates the islands of Java and Sumatra.

questions.question: Which of the following types of rocks is formed from the cooling and solidification of magma?
questions.explanation: Igneous rocks are formed from the cooling and solidification of magma.

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of people from rural areas to cities?
questions.explanation: Urbanization is the movement of people from rural areas to cities.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest desert in Asia?
questions.explanation: The Gobi desert is the largest desert in Asia.

questions.question: What is the primary factor influencing the formation of monsoon patterns in India?
questions.explanation: Pressure differences between land and sea drive monsoon patterns. Low pressure over land and high pressure over the ocean create a pressure gradient.

questions.question: What is the name of the strait separating the islands of Java and Sumatra in Indonesia?
questions.explanation: The Sunda Strait is a major shipping route between the Indian and Pacific Oceans.

questions.question: Which river is the longest in South America and flows through Brazil, Peru, and Colombia?
questions.explanation: The Amazon River is approximately 6,400 kilometers long and is the largest river in the world by discharge volume.

questions.question: Which desert is located in the northwestern part of India and covers parts of Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Haryana?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert is also known as the Cholistan Desert and covers an area of approximately 260,000 square kilometers.

questions.question: Which type of rock is formed from the cooling and solidification of magma or lava?
questions.explanation: Igneous rocks can be either intrusive or extrusive, depending on where the cooling and solidification occur.

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of people from rural areas to cities, resulting in urban growth?
questions.explanation: Urbanization is a major demographic trend in many countries and can lead to social, economic, and environmental changes.

questions.question: Which climate zone is characterized by high temperatures and high levels of rainfall throughout the year?
questions.explanation: Tropical climates are found near the equator and have minimal seasonal variation in temperature.

questions.question: What is the name of the strait separating the islands of Java and Sumatra?
questions.explanation: The Sunda Strait connects the Java Sea to the Indian Ocean.

questions.question: Which river is the longest in South India?
questions.explanation: The Godavari River flows through several states in South India.

questions.question: What is the name of the desert located in the northwestern part of India?
questions.explanation: The Thar Desert is also known as the Cholistan Desert.

questions.question: Which type of climate is characterized by high temperatures and high levels of precipitation?
questions.explanation: Tropical climates are typically hot and humid.

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of the Earth's crust?
questions.explanation: Plate tectonics is the theory that the Earth's crust is divided into moving plates.

questions.question: Which river flows through the Grand Canyon?
questions.explanation: The Colorado River is responsible for carving the Grand Canyon.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is not a part of the Ganga river system?
questions.explanation: Narmada river is not part of the Ganga river system.

questions.question: What is the process by which water moves through a plant, from the roots to the atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the process of water movement through plants.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of natural resource?
questions.explanation: Manufactured goods are not natural resources.

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of people from rural to urban areas?
questions.explanation: Urbanization refers to the movement from rural to urban areas.

questions.question: What is the largest desert in the world?
questions.explanation: Antarctic desert is the largest desert in the world.

questions.question: Which of the following river systems is known for its unique phenomenon of reversing flow direction seasonally?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra river's flow direction changes due to varying water levels and siltation.

questions.question: What term describes the process by which the Earth's rotational force influences global wind patterns and ocean currents?
questions.explanation: Coriolis effect is the apparent deflection of moving objects on Earth due to the planet's rotation.

questions.question: Which mountain range acts as a barrier to the movement of monsoon winds into India from the southwest?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas force the winds to rise and cool, resulting in precipitation.

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of water from the ground surface to the atmosphere through plants?
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the process of water movement through plants and evaporation from aerial parts.

questions.question: Which strait separates the Indian subcontinent from Sri Lanka?
questions.explanation: The Palk Strait connects the Bay of Bengal to the Indian Ocean.

questions.question: What type of climate is characterized by low precipitation and high evaporation rates?
questions.explanation: Desert climates have limited rainfall and high evaporation due to intense sunlight.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a factor influencing the formation of natural resources like fossil fuels?
questions.explanation: Gravity does not directly influence fossil fuel formation, which is driven by heat, pressure, and time.

questions.question: What term describes the large-scale movement of water from the ocean to the atmosphere through evaporation?
questions.explanation: Evapotranspiration includes both evaporation from the ocean and transpiration from plants.

questions.question: Which of the following natural resources is renewable and can be replenished naturally?
questions.explanation: Water is a renewable resource as it can be replenished through the water cycle.

questions.question: What is the primary cause of the formation of the monsoon trough in India?
questions.explanation: The primary cause of the formation of the monsoon trough in India is the temperature gradient between the land and sea.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers flows through the rift valley of the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra river flows through the rift valley of the Himalayas.

questions.question: What is the primary factor influencing the formation of monsoon patterns in the Indian subcontinent, and which mountain range plays a crucial role in this process?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas play a crucial role in the formation of monsoon patterns in the Indian subcontinent by blocking and deflecting wind masses. This orographic lift leads to precipitation.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is known for being the longest in South Asia and flows through which countries?
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra is the longest river in South Asia and flows through India, China, and Bangladesh.

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of water from the ground to the atmosphere through plants, and which process is primarily responsible for this phenomenon?
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the process by which water is transported through a plant, from the roots to the atmosphere, primarily through stomata.

questions.question: What is the primary cause of the formation of the Indian monsoon, and which mountain range plays a significant role in its formation?
questions.explanation: The Himalayan Range plays a significant role in the formation of the Indian monsoon, as it forces the winds to rise and cool, resulting in precipitation.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is the longest in India, and which city is it connected to via a major canal?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is the longest river in India and is connected to Delhi via the Ganga Canal.

questions.question: What is the term for the process by which the Earth's plates move, resulting in the formation of mountain ranges such as the Himalayas?
questions.explanation: Plate Tectonics is the process by which the Earth's plates move, resulting in the formation of mountain ranges.

questions.question: Which strait separates the islands of Java and Sumatra, and which ocean is it connected to?
questions.explanation: The Sunda Strait separates the islands of Java and Sumatra and is connected to the Indian Ocean.

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of water from the ground to the atmosphere through plants, and which process is it a part of?
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the movement of water from the ground to the atmosphere through plants and is a part of the water cycle.

questions.question: Which of the following types of climate is characterized by high temperatures and high levels of rainfall, and which region of India experiences this type of climate?
questions.explanation: Tropical climate is characterized by high temperatures and high levels of rainfall and is experienced in regions such as Kerala.

questions.question: What is the term for the process by which rocks are broken down into smaller particles, and which type of rock is most susceptible to this process?
questions.explanation: Weathering is the process by which rocks are broken down into smaller particles and is most susceptible in sedimentary rocks.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain ranges runs along the border between India and Pakistan, and which pass is located in this range?
questions.explanation: The Karakoram range runs along the border between India and Pakistan and is home to the Banihal Pass.

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of people from rural to urban areas, and which city in India has experienced the highest rate of urbanization?
questions.explanation: Urbanization is the movement of people from rural to urban areas and Delhi has experienced the highest rate of urbanization.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers flows through the Grand Canyon, and which state in India has a similar type of canyon?
questions.explanation: The Colorado River flows through the Grand Canyon and the Narmada River in India has a similar type of canyon.

questions.question: What is the primary cause of the formation of the monsoon trough in India during the summer months, and which mountain range runs parallel to this trough, and what is the name of the process by which the monsoon trough is formed?
questions.explanation: The monsoon trough is formed due to convergence of winds, Himalayas run parallel to it.

questions.question: Which river is the longest in South India, and which city is it connected to via a man-made canal, and what is the name of the strait that separates the Indian subcontinent from Sri Lanka?
questions.explanation: Palk Strait separates India from Sri Lanka.

questions.question: What is the term used to describe the movement of the Earth's crust that resulted in the formation of the Himalayan mountain range, and which type of rocks are found in this range, and what is the name of the process that occurs when the Indian plate collides with the Eurasian plate?
questions.explanation: Orogeny is the process of mountain formation.

questions.question: Which type of climate is characterized by high temperatures and high levels of rainfall throughout the year, and which region in India experiences this type of climate, and what is the term used to describe this type of climate?
questions.explanation: Tropical climate is characterized by high temperatures and rainfall.

questions.question: What is the name of the process by which water moves through a plant, from the roots to the leaves, and is then released into the air as water vapor, and which type of forest is characterized by a high level of this process, and what is the term used to describe this process?
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the process of water movement through plants.

questions.question: Which type of rock is formed from the cooling and solidification of magma, and which type of landform is characterized by the presence of this type of rock, and what is the term used to describe this type of rock?
questions.explanation: Igneous rock is formed from cooling magma.

questions.question: What is the term used to describe the movement of people from rural areas to cities, and which type of city is characterized by a high level of this movement, and what is the term used to describe this type of city?
questions.explanation: Urbanization is the movement of people from rural to urban areas.

questions.question: Which type of natural hazard is characterized by the movement of the Earth's crust, and which type of wave is produced by this movement, and what is the term used to describe this type of hazard?
questions.explanation: Earthquake is a natural hazard caused by crust movement.

questions.question: What is the term used to describe the process by which the universe became transparent to light, and which type of radiation is emitted during this process, and what is the term used to describe this type of radiation?
questions.explanation: Recombination is the process of universe becoming transparent.

questions.question: Which type of ecosystem is characterized by a high level of biodiversity, and which type of forest is an example of this ecosystem, and what is the term used to describe this type of ecosystem?
questions.explanation: Rainforest is an example of a diverse ecosystem.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is the longest in India and flows through which major strait?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is the longest river in India and flows through the Sundarbans delta.

questions.question: What is the process by which water moves through a plant, from the roots to the atmosphere, and which type of forest is most affected by this process?
questions.explanation: Transpiration is the process by which water moves through a plant, and tropical forests are most affected by this process.

questions.question: Which type of rock is formed from the cooling and solidification of magma, and which type of mountain is composed of this rock?
questions.explanation: Igneous rock is formed from the cooling and solidification of magma, and volcanic mountains are composed of this rock.

questions.question: What is the term for the movement of the Earth's crust, and which type of boundary is characterized by the creation of new crust?
questions.explanation: Plate Tectonics is the term for the movement of the Earth's crust, and divergent boundaries are characterized by the creation of new crust.

questions.question: Which of the following types of climate is characterized by high temperatures and high levels of rainfall, and which type of vegetation is typically found in this climate?
questions.explanation: Tropical climate is characterized by high temperatures and high levels of rainfall, and rainforests are typically found in this climate.

questions.question: What is the term for the process by which the Earth's surface is worn away, and which type of landscape is typically formed by this process?
questions.explanation: Erosion is the term for the process by which the Earth's surface is worn away, and valleys are typically formed by this process.
